comment: Ott obviously knows the material but gives incomplete explanations or trails off his sentences. Two quizzes, four large homework assignments, and four labs that are too big to be completed in class. He just doesn't require some of the questions. There are three textbooks for the class, all of which he provides for free. Two of them he wrote.

It is a very strange class. The professor tried hard to teach, but he had a strong accent. The first midterm exam was incredibly difficult. The second midterm exam was a direct copy of the practice exam, which was baffling. The weekly assignments consisted of at least dozens of problems, which were very time-consuming. He could organized it better.

He made the course material easy to follow and understand. The lectures were very helpful, and the homework was never too difficult. The provided textbook was great too. He always gave us time in class to work on homework and was available for questions.
Good teacher. Class is well structured with an easy to follow textbook. Quizzes can be challenging, but aren't too hard if you prepare using the textbook.
Weird class. Boring lectures, you won't learn anything and he'll take quizzes harder than cse 12X series. Honestly, the TA William Wang was the main problem but a really bad experience.
Attendance: Not Mandatory

Ott is a great professor and always answers questions quickly. However, the lectures are a bit boring. The lab and homework were reasonable. I heard that this class would be easy, but thanks to the lead TA, William Wang, we now have two tests worth a lot. The first one was incredibly difficult, and there isn't a curve for the second test.

Ott is a good professor and gives relatively good lectures, but the course organization is wack. There's a good bit of homework each week, and the assignments are extremely long and often contain confusing/conflicting information. When asking the TAs, it feels like they don't even know. I coded more for this class than for my CSE123 class.

A professor actually resorted to such a despicable trick—inserting invisible hints into assignments to confuse the AI. I have no idea what his intentions were. This kind of thing should be discussed openly, not done through such underhanded tactics.

He speaks with a heavy accent, making him difficult to understand most of the time. There's always a lot of homework. The course organization also has some issues; he introduced exams for the first time, and the exams were incredibly difficult.

Ott is a great lecturer and genuinely kind. But if your TA is William Wang, run. He swapped the project-based format for two big “quizzes” and forced hand-written code (in 2025!), spiking workload and penalizing tiny syntax slips. Content fine; delivery not. Avoid that TA or another section.
This guy is fricking awesome. Very easy to follow lectures and, through discord, super accessible and always available to help with questions. I love this guy!
Genuinely awful class, he didn't explain concepts clearly and he outright skipped going over Git and the TAs did not help students at all.

Great class for new or experienced programmers. The problem sets are a little tedious, so try not to procrastinate them. Consider doing the extra credit for the earlier assignments while the class is easier compared to later ones. I really enjoyed this class and found Ott as a great professor.

5 stars for Ott!!! Took this course for analytics requirement for Real Estate - LOVED it. Great format, finals week was nice. There's weekly problem sets, labs where you work in a team, and then the final is a group project. Ott is FUN and I learned a lot.

It's a pretty easy, chill class. Sometimes lectures can feel a little slow, but Ott really wants to make sure every student understands what's going on. There's only one Problem Set a week, and several labs are optional. He's also very accessible on Discord for questions.

I loved Ott! The lectures can be boring but he is always willing to help during his office hours and through Discord. Weekly assignments that takes up a few hours to complete if you're new to coding but it's doable and the amount of work is fair. There's group work for the final project but it's easy if you have a good group.

I didn't have any coding experience before entering this class and feel like I gained a lot through taking INFO 201. Ott has a playful sense of humor and makes the long lectures enjoyable. This is a solid intro class.

The class was interesting and I did learn a lot about R and data analysis. But when I had a lab question Ott was extremely unhelpful in office hours and made me feel like I wasn't intelligent for not understanding the problem. I only figured it out when another classmate came in and we worked together instead. Didn't try office hours after that.

professor ott is a good professor - he and his ta's are easily accessible on campus and online. the only thing is there is a lot of work that he assigns (both problem sets + lab sets) which feels really overwhelming.. it just feels extremely fast-paced and of course its different for everyone but for me, it is a very difficult intro class imo.

Ott is great! Although a 2-hour lecture isn't really justifiable, I really enjoy his structure and material. There is about 3 reading quiz (online), 6 programming assignments and couple of lab assignments and group project. Ott try his best to answer any question 24/7 on discord. Overall, I really enjoy this class and learning R!
Attendance: Not Mandatory
Professor Toomet is so nice love him definitely would take his class again.

Pretty easy class whether you have prior coding experience or not. Starts super slow, but does start ramping up a bit (content is still very accessible and self-learnable though). Textbook and assignments are sometimes a little unclear, but grading is quite lenient. There are no real tests; you'll just have a few problem sets and a final project.

For those with no coding xp, this class is not easy. W the class being redone, Ott had to rewrite all of the assignments, so there were sometimes unclear instructions. Assignments are long, but Ott and his TAs are very accessible outside of class via the Discord at almost every time of the day, even late at night. Hard class, but helpful professor.

Such a nice and caring professor and his team of TA is just as good! its extremely nice that he is accessible outside through discord and is active to help students with code issues. I would def take a class with him again. The HW is once a week due, but can be time consuming, but if u can plan it out youll be fine

Course is being re-done, sadly assignments had confusing, ambiguous questions. Assignments take time to complete but are interesting. Ott welcomes feedback, helps students on Discord with questions constantly. Easy class (with 6 years coding experience), but can be time-consuming. Thankfully, TAs don't grade on code efficiency.

INFO 371 is more difficult than 370, and has more complex homework. However, Ott does a great job of explaining concepts and is very accessible in office hours. This class allows group work on some projects, which is helpful for the last problem sets. Take this class with Ott!

Ott was great at demonstrating concepts in his lecture demos and was pretty funny at times. Problem sets were challenging but he is always on the discord answering student questions along with the TAs. Class got me interested in data and data programming, I would recommend!

Prof. Ott is awesome, and I definitely recommend him for the 370-371 INFO series. His lectures are very informative, and he only gives one small quiz a quarter. Your grade comes from 7-8 assignments, and they are directly related to the lectures. As long as you pay attention in class you'll get an easy A. One of my favorite professors in iSchool.
The best professor I've ever had within my 3 years of college (I am a junior).

Ott is amazing and gives fast and helpful feedback outside class. His lecture is organized and goes in a slow pace. He explains things carefully. Homework load is ok and not hard. Homeworks comes with instructions and it is a good way to learn. Textbooks and other resources are all free.

Ott was super nice and enthusiastic. The labs were chill and directly applied to what was taught in class. Group projects were similar to labs and very manageable. Ott and the TAs provided tons of office hours and help through Discord. I'm not familiar with coding (or really anything tech-related) but had plenty of resources to succeed!
Class was super easy but Ott was still enthusiastic about it. pretty easy projects and he was more than happy to help with them through discord.
You can feel that he cares about students are really learning, not just grading stuff. I will totally take his class!

I took the info 180 class (not info 201). His lectures were incredibly dry. He repeats too much and his pacing is ridiculous. You can tell he cares about his students, but those 2 hours felt like an eternity. His assignments are very easy though. Group work, bi-weekly labs, and 2 tests (no midterms or finals). Easy class, but mind-numbing lectures.

Ott is a great professor. He was willing to change due dates if a lot of the class was falling behind, and was quick to respond to students on Teams. Just ask if you need anything, and he'll help if he can. He was also funny in lectures, so they weren't boring. He does have a bit of an accent, but I was able to understand him just fine.
Can we please improve Ott's rating? His classes are honestly my favorite at UW. Compassionate, patient, and intelligent!

Awesome professor for INFO 370/371. Easily approachable and very amiable! Ott's lecture notes and textbook offer rich contexts to complete assignments and replies questions in a timely manner. He genuinely cares for his students and wants them to get the most out of his courses!

Amazing professor for INFO 370/371. Very knowledgeable and approachable. Ott really cares about his students and he genuinely wants us to learn. His lecture notes/python notes are super helpful, and he is also extremely supportive during office hours or outside of class as he would always provide thorough explanations to students' questions.

Ott is one of the nicest proffesors you will ever meet. Really cares that students do well in his course and is always incredible helpful in office hours. His lectures are slow, but it's perfect for difficult, programming oriented courses like the data science series.

Ott is an amazing professor who truly cares about his student. He goes pretty slow on the lectures so you can understand the content well. You oftentimes can find him answering students' questions on Teams at 10pm. The rate my professor score does not reflect his course at all, in a way, students might be biased against his accent.

Ott genuinely wants you to pass his class. He joined the discord server to answer all our questions even during the middle of the night. Not just give you the answer, but will make sure you are understanding the concepts. Homework can be a little challenging but doable if you watch lectures since he does similar problems during lecture. Helpful OH.

Took this class with no coding background and 4.0'd. Ott did a great job answering questions and being thorough with lots of examples since TAs were not helpful. I went to the lectures but no quiz section. HW was challenging but doable if you took the time to understand the lecture. The final project was hard but the grading was lenient.

Professor Toomet is one of those professors who genuinely wants his students to learn and succeed. Joined us all on Discord and would regularly answer questions anybody had (even students who had the other prof). Ive literally messaged this guy in the middle of the night and she still answered and helped me on my homework :).

I have never had a professor as caring as Ott. He has many times been up til 11PM helping any student that needs help, so if you're stuck, you know you can just message him and he will quickly reply. He won't just give you answers, but walk you through the kind of thinking you need to do in order to understand the concepts. Great lecture style too!

Amazing professor for INFO 370. People say his lectures are a bit slow, but his pacing is perfect when covering difficult material. He gives very clear explanations and is incredibly helpful in office hours.

Students asked many basic questions, but Ott patiently and thoroughly answered all of them. He was also very accommodating and extended deadlines and provided extra credit. Ott really cares about the success of his students. I learned a ton in his class. I went from knowing nothing about R to being quite proficient.

Seems like a lot of people here didn't do well in INFO201 and thus a 1/5 doxx. Ott does NOT deserve this low of a score. His lecture style is exactly how I learn best, and he is one of the sweetest profs I've ever had. Took 370 last quarter and am taking 371 with him right now. Even if I feel too tired to attend class I go for him!

It's the ambiguity in his assignments that takes off points, nothing with difficulty. One example is a R assignment where we examined a simple linear regression for 5 variables, one at a time. Then it asked for a multi-linear-regression using "all variables". This isn't the 5 used previously at once but all variables in the dataset. Happens often.

Ott is an amazing professor and extremely knowledgeable in the realm of data science. His lectures can be dry sometimes hence many don't get to learn a lot, but he is extremely helpful during office hours. Ott will make sure you have understood the concept completely and help you with your homework. Overall, okayish professor, amazing resource

Ott has quite a few bad reviews and while I personally had an okay time in his class I do understand where the complaints are coming from. That said I don't believe he deserves as low of a score as he has on here and is actually quite helpful in providing help to students that ask for it. Overall a tough but fair teacher.

People don't like Ott because of his accent and sometimes dry lectures, but if there is a teacher at this university that cares about your learning more than the grade, it's Ott. He repeatedly will give opportunities to raise grades as long as you put in effort to learn the content. On top of that, he's always available and loves to help.

He regularly allows students to ask questions and clarify their doubts, often repeating himself numerous times. There are also always many TAs ready to help! Most people just don't show up for lecture and that's why they are confused!
The course content is all inside the book. Just forget about going to lecture and ask everything on Slack & read the FREE online textbook which is really detailed and you should do fine.

Ott is nice, but his lectures can be pretty boring sometimes. They are basically demo sessions of the knowledge in the course book. Mostly projects, you can do well by reading the book and using Google. I do recommend going to his office hours, he can give you a more concise answer than in lecture, and he teaches you some useful tricks also.
His lecture is poor structured, it's very hard to follow. The homework specification is poor worded, sometimes i don't even know what i should do. He is good guy but not a great instructor.

This class is supposed to be easy, except if you take it with Ott. Nice guy but if you cant take it with Ross or Freeman dont take it at all. This class was harder for me than CSE142 just because of the way it was taught. I was miserable all quarter and got a grade way lower than I wouldve gotten anyway. If you are reading this run, run far away

I have never been more confused in a class. The material should be easy and do able but you are not taught anything and have to spend the entire class Googling how to do everything. Take this class with somebody else unless you want to make your life way harder than it needs to be.
Too heavy on statistics. Professor assumes students to know all the concepts. Not for all.

Ott wants you to know how things work as he tells you how to do things from a terminal (whereas many people just tap on a button). He does not cover all the slides and his examples are somehow different, so you'd better take notes of both. And please, install Linux for his class (at least in a VM), because his demo doesn't always work on Windows.
Nice teacher but hard course
this class is horrible dont take it if u dont have to. worse than any cs coding class ive even taken at UW. Ott is just not a good prof. hard to understand and follow in the lecture
NO SPECIFIC GUIDELINE FOR HOMEWORK

The homework takes a really long time. The lectures are somewhat related to the homework, but not quite, which sucks because the majority of your grade is from the homework. You have to see help and do a lot of Googling to get the homework done. The online textbooks kinda help, but you really on your own.

Lots of HW but it's due one every week. Lecture heavy. Online textbook THAT'S FREE. Miss a lecture and you'll get lost if you're new to programming anything. Kind and wants to help but you have to seek it yourself.
